mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample backtests spanning 2000-2005.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising real residential construction starts and a stable federal funds rate indicates accommodative financial conditions supporting corporate capex, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: stable monetary policy with housing-led recovery
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a coefficient with p > 0.10, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high HOUST/low FEDFUNDS regimes and other regimes.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, a simultaneous contraction in consumer credit signals demand exhaustion rather than supply-driven expansion, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle supply expansion with credit contraction
failure_condition: The cross-sectional momentum of industrial stocks does not decline when credit contracts, or forward returns remain positive despite the specified trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 195.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when CPI crosses the threshold, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in out-of-sample tests covering 2003-2005.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising employment and flat industrial production indicates a productivity-driven expansion that supports corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient, or forward returns do not outperform during periods of rising employment with stagnant output.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where housing starts peak and unemployment remains below 5.5%, consumer demand becomes unsustainable, triggering a mean-reversion in equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with peak housing and tight labor
failure_condition: Equity valuations do not compress when housing peaks alongside low unemployment, or forward returns remain positive despite the specified trigger condition.
